prompt: The rule of Conjunction (∧-introduction) lets you infer:
options: P ∧ Q, given P and Q on separate lines
Q, given P → Q and P
hint: Conjunction BUILDS an 'and' from two things you already have.
explanation: Conjunction (∧-introduction): from P and Q (each established) infer P ∧ Q. Option 2 is Simplification (∧-elimination), option 3 is Addition (∨-introduction), and option 4 is Modus Ponens.
